text:
HEMATOLOGY/ONCOLOGY — BONE MARROW TRANSPLANT PROGRAM
PRE-TRANSPLANT EVALUATION AND CONSULTATION NOTE

PATIENT: Marcus T. Delgado
AGE: 48 years
SEX: Male
ATTENDING: BMT Attending Physician
SERVICE: Allogeneic Stem Cell Transplant Program

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Mr. Delgado is a 48-year-old male high school science teacher presenting for comprehensive pre-transplant evaluation in anticipation of myeloablative allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT) for AML in first complete remission.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:

Mr. Delgado is a 48-year-old non-smoking male with a history of well-controlled hypertension who was diagnosed approximately six weeks ago with de novo acute myeloid leukemia (AML), NOS — specifically AML without maturation (FAB M1). His presentation was notable for a three-week prodrome of progressive fatigue, pallor, easy bruising, spontaneous gingival bleeding with gingival hypertrophy, and occasional epistaxis. At the time of diagnosis, his presenting CBC was remarkable for a WBC of 84,000/uL, hemoglobin of 7.2 g/dL, and platelets of 28,000/uL, with 62% circulating blasts on peripheral smear. Bone marrow biopsy confirmed markedly hypercellular marrow with 78% blasts. Karyotype was normal (46,XY diploid) on conventional G-band cytogenetics. Comprehensive myeloid NGS panel (283-gene) identified a FLT3 internal tandem duplication (ITD) at the exon 14-15 junction with a high allelic ratio of 0.87 (VAF 47%), and a co-occurring DNMT3A p.R882H mutation (VAF 42%); NPM1 was wild-type. Per ELN 2022 criteria, he was stratified as adverse-risk AML, largely driven by the FLT3-ITD with high allelic ratio in the absence of NPM1 co-mutation. No cytogenetic high-risk features, KMT2A rearrangement, TP53 mutation, or monosomal karyotype were identified. CT staging at diagnosis showed mild hepatosplenomegaly consistent with leukemic infiltration; no extramedullary disease or chloroma was identified.

He was admitted and initiated on induction chemotherapy with the 7+3 regimen: cytarabine 100 mg/m2/day by continuous IV infusion days 1-7, daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 IV bolus days 1-3, and midostaurin 50 mg PO BID days 8-21 per the RATIFY protocol. His hospital course was complicated by expected post-induction febrile neutropenia (ANC nadir 0, day 14; blood cultures negative, responded to empiric cefepime), grade 2 oral mucositis, and grade 2 nausea/vomiting. Midostaurin was briefly held for 5 days due to nausea and then restarted at the same dose. ANC recovery to >1,000/uL was achieved on day 28.

Day 28-30 remission assessment bone marrow biopsy confirmed complete morphologic remission (CR1): marrow markedly hypocellular with trilineage recovery underway, blasts <5% by morphology. FLT3-ITD PCR was NEGATIVE. Multiparameter flow cytometry MRD (10-color panel, sensitivity 10^-4) was NEGATIVE — no leukemia-associated immunophenotype detected. Repeat CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis showed resolution of the prior hepatosplenomegaly with no evidence of extramedullary disease. Peripheral blood counts at day 30-32 follow-up: WBC 3.8 k/uL, ANC 1,820/uL, Hgb 10.4 g/dL, platelets 82,000/uL, continuing to recover.

Given the ELN adverse-risk profile (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, DNMT3A R882H co-mutation, NPM1 wild-type), allogeneic HSCT in CR1 is strongly recommended per current guidelines and institutional practice. HLA typing was submitted at diagnosis (10-locus high-resolution typing: HLA-A, B, C, DRB1, DQB1). A 10/10 HLA-allele level matched unrelated donor (MUD) has been identified through the NMDP registry — a CMV-seropositive male donor. No consolidation chemotherapy was administered prior to transplant evaluation, as the MRD-negative CR1 and prompt donor availability make early transplant the preferred approach. The patient presents today for his formal pre-transplant work-up and organ function assessment.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1. Acute myeloid leukemia, NOS (AML without maturation, FAB M1), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, DNMT3A R882H — diagnosed approximately 6 weeks ago, currently in CR1 MRD-negative post-7+3 plus midostaurin induction
2. Hypertension — well-controlled on lisinopril 10 mg daily; no end-organ damage documented
3. No prior hematologic conditions
4. No prior malignancy
5. No prior radiation exposure
6. No diabetes mellitus
7. No known cardiac disease prior to AML diagnosis; echocardiogram at AML diagnosis showed EF 62%, no wall motion abnormalities

PAST SURGICAL HISTORY:
- PICC line placement for induction chemotherapy (recently removed)
- Bilateral bone marrow biopsies x2 (diagnostic at presentation; day 28-30 remission assessment)
- Lumbar puncture at diagnosis (CSF cytology negative for blasts)

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Maternal uncle: colon cancer, diagnosed at age 62
- No family history of hematologic malignancy, lymphoma, or leukemia
- No known family history of bone marrow failure syndromes or inherited predisposition syndromes (Fanconi anemia, dyskeratosis congenita, GATA2 deficiency)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Occupation: high school science teacher; currently on medical leave
- Tobacco: never smoker
- Alcohol: social use only, minimal; none during treatment
- Illicit substances: none
- Marital status: married
- Lives with spouse; supportive home environment
- Insurance: verified; transplant pre-authorization initiated
- Caregiver: spouse available as primary caregiver
- Housing: lives approximately 45 minutes from transplant center; will arrange temporary lodging nearby for the post-transplant period

ALLERGIES:
- No known drug allergies
- No known food allergies

MEDICATIONS (current at time of evaluation):
1. Lisinopril 10 mg PO daily (hypertension)
2. Fluconazole 400 mg PO daily (antifungal prophylaxis, continued post-induction)
3. Acyclovir 400 mg PO BID (antiviral prophylaxis)
4. Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (TMP-SMX) 1 DS tablet PO three times weekly (PCP prophylaxis)
5. Levofloxacin — completed (was on during induction neutropenia, now off given ANC recovery)
6. Midostaurin — held at time of evaluation pending transplant center recommendation regarding continuation as bridge to transplant
7. Ondansetron 8 mg PO PRN nausea
8. Allopurinol — completed (discontinued after tumor lysis risk period resolved)

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:

Constitutional: Fatigue present but significantly improved from presentation; reports energy level approximately 70% of baseline. No fever, chills, or night sweats. Weight has returned toward baseline following initial 4-5 lb weight loss during induction hospitalization.
Head/Eyes/Ears/Nose/Throat: Gingival hypertrophy and bleeding fully resolved. Mild residual mouth dryness, no oral mucositis at present. No epistaxis. No visual changes.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or dyspnea on exertion. No lower extremity edema. No orthopnea.
Pulmonary: No cough, shortness of breath, or wheezing. No hemoptysis.
Gastrointestinal: Appetite has returned to approximately 80% of baseline. Mild nausea occasionally post-midostaurin dose (prior to hold); no vomiting currently. Bowel function normal.
Genitourinary: Normal urinary function. No hematuria.
Musculoskeletal: No bone pain. No arthralgias.
Skin: No rash. No petechiae or ecchymoses.
Neurologic: No headache, dizziness, visual changes, numbness, or weakness. No focal neurologic symptoms.
Psychosocial: Patient reports significant anxiety regarding transplant and long-term outcome, though motivated and engaged. Has discussed prognosis with spouse. No current depression based on screening.
Endocrine: No hot flashes, no symptoms of hypothyroidism.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:

Vital Signs: Temperature 36.8°C, BP 130/80 mmHg, HR 78 bpm, RR 14 breaths/min, O2 saturation 98% on room air, weight 78 kg, height 180 cm, BSA approximately 1.96 m2. KPS 80 (ambulatory, able to carry on normal activity with effort; some signs or symptoms of disease).

General: Well-appearing male in no acute distress. Alert, oriented. Mildly fatigued-appearing but conversant and engaged.

Head: Normocephalic, atraumatic.

Eyes: Pupils equal and reactive. Sclerae non-icteric. Conjunctivae mildly pale.

Oral/Pharyngeal: Oral mucosa moist and intact. No mucositis lesions. Gingival tissue normalizing — no hypertrophy or active bleeding present. No oral thrush. Dentition reviewed per dental clearance (completed, no active dental infection or source of bacteremia identified).

Neck/Lymph nodes: No cervical, supraclavicular, or axillary lymphadenopathy. Neck supple.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1 and S2. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No JVD. No peripheral edema.

Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes, crackles, or rhonchi. Good air movement throughout.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly (liver edge not palpable). Spleen not palpable. No ascites. Bowel sounds normal.

Extremities: No clubbing, cyanosis, or edema. PICC site healed without erythema or induration.

Skin: No rash, petechiae, or purpura. No skin infiltration. No bruising.

Neurologic: Alert and oriented x3. Cranial nerves II-XII intact. No focal motor or sensory deficits. Coordination intact.

---

LAB RESULTS (Pre-Transplant Evaluation):

Complete Blood Count:
- WBC: 5.4 k/uL
- ANC: 3,200/uL
- Hemoglobin: 11.2 g/dL
- Hematocrit: 33.8%
- MCV: 90 fL
- Platelets: 124,000/uL
- Differential: neutrophils 59%, lymphocytes 28%, monocytes 10%, eosinophils 3%, no blasts identified

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium: 139 mEq/L
- Potassium: 4.1 mEq/L
- Creatinine: 0.9 mg/dL (GFR >80 mL/min/1.73m2 by CKD-EPI)
- BUN: 14 mg/dL
- Glucose: 98 mg/dL
- Total bilirubin: 0.7 mg/dL
- Direct bilirubin: 0.2 mg/dL
- AST: 28 U/L (within normal limits)
- ALT: 32 U/L (within normal limits)
- Alkaline phosphatase: 74 U/L
- Albumin: 3.8 g/dL
- Total protein: 6.9 g/dL
- LDH: 198 U/L (normalized from 1,240 U/L at diagnosis)

Coagulation:
- PT/INR: 1.1
- aPTT: 28 seconds

Infectious Disease Serology (transplant screen):
- CMV IgG: POSITIVE (seropositive, R+)
- EBV IgG: POSITIVE (seropositive)
- HSV IgG: POSITIVE (seropositive)
- VZV IgG: POSITIVE (seropositive)
- HIV 1/2 antigen/antibody: NEGATIVE
- Hepatitis B surface antigen: NEGATIVE
- Hepatitis B surface antibody: positive (likely from prior vaccination)
- Hepatitis B core antibody (total): NEGATIVE
- Hepatitis C antibody: NEGATIVE
- RPR/VDRL: non-reactive
- Toxoplasma IgG: NEGATIVE
- HTLV I/II: NEGATIVE

Thyroid:
- TSH: 2.1 mIU/L (normal)

Ferritin: 1,840 ng/mL (elevated, consistent with transfusion history and systemic inflammation during induction; will be monitored post-transplant for iron overload)

Molecular/MRD:
- FLT3-ITD PCR from peripheral blood: NEGATIVE (confirmed at day 30-32 post-induction)

---

IMAGING / DIAGNOSTIC STUDIES:

Echocardiogram (repeat, performed today):
- Left ventricular ejection fraction: 60% (preserved; baseline at diagnosis was 62%)
- No wall motion abnormalities
- No pericardial effusion
- Mild trace tricuspid regurgitation — not hemodynamically significant
- Conclusion: Cardiac function preserved, acceptable for myeloablative conditioning with anthracycline-based pre-transplant workup complete

Pulmonary Function Tests (performed today):
- FVC: 4.52 L (91% predicted)
- FEV1: 3.98 L (88% predicted)
- FEV1/FVC ratio: 0.88 (normal)
- DLCO corrected: 82% predicted
- TLC: 6.4 L (92% predicted)
- Conclusion: Pulmonary function within acceptable parameters for myeloablative conditioning; no obstructive or restrictive defect

Prior CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis (day ~30 post-induction):
- Reviewed: complete resolution of prior hepatosplenomegaly; no lymphadenopathy; no extramedullary disease

---

ASSESSMENT:

1. Acute myeloid leukemia (AML), NOS — FAB M1, de novo; FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio (AR 0.87), DNMT3A R882H co-mutation; normal diploid karyotype; ELN 2022 adverse-risk classification — currently in MRD-negative complete remission (CR1) following 7+3 induction plus midostaurin

Mr. Delgado has had an excellent initial response to induction chemotherapy, achieving morphologic CR1 with MRD negativity by both multiparameter flow cytometry and FLT3-ITD PCR. However, his disease biology is high-risk: FLT3-ITD with a very high allelic ratio of 0.87 in the absence of an NPM1 co-mutation confers a significantly elevated relapse risk, and DNMT3A R882H is an adverse co-mutation associated with clonal persistence. The combination of these features places him squarely in the ELN adverse-risk category where allogeneic transplant in CR1 is the standard of care supported by multiple prospective trials and NCCN/ELN guidelines. His current MRD-negative status in CR1 represents the optimal window for transplant.

2. Pre-transplant organ function assessment — acceptable for myeloablative conditioning

All organ function parameters are within acceptable ranges: cardiac (EF 60%, no wall motion abnormality), pulmonary (FEV1 88%, DLCO 82%), renal (Cr 0.9, GFR >80), and hepatic (normal transaminases, bilirubin 0.7). No contraindications to myeloablative busulfan-fludarabine conditioning identified.

3. Donor identified: 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor (MUD), CMV-seropositive male, through NMDP

Donor work-up and confirmation pending. Peripheral blood stem cell graft with G-CSF mobilization planned. Target CD34+ cell dose ≥5 x 10^6/kg.

4. Infectious disease risk profile: CMV R+/D+ — highest risk group for CMV reactivation post-transplant; weekly CMV PCR monitoring protocol to be implemented

5. Elevated ferritin — monitor post-transplant; therapeutic phlebotomy or chelation may be considered if ferritin remains elevated at 3-6 months post-transplant and engraftment is fully established

---

PLAN:

Transplant Conditioning Regimen:
Myeloablative conditioning with fludarabine 30 mg/m2 IV daily on days -6, -5, -4, -3, and -2 (5 doses), and busulfan 3.2 mg/kg IV daily on days -5, -4, -3, and -2 (4 doses; FluBu4). Busulfan will be dosed with pharmacokinetic monitoring targeting an AUC of 5,000-6,000 uM*min/day to minimize toxicity while ensuring myeloablation; dose adjustments will be made based on first-dose PK sampling. Seizure prophylaxis with levetiracetam will be administered throughout busulfan dosing per protocol.

GVHD Prophylaxis:
Tacrolimus (starting day -3, target trough 10-15 ng/mL) plus short-course methotrexate (15 mg/m2 IV day +1; 10 mg/m2 IV days +3, +6, +11). No in vivo T-cell depletion (ATG not planned given availability of well-matched unrelated donor and desire to preserve graft-versus-leukemia effect given adverse-risk AML). Patient counseled extensively regarding GVHD risks (both acute and chronic), including skin, GI, hepatic, pulmonary, and other organ involvement.

Antimicrobial Prophylaxis (to be resumed/continued during conditioning and post-transplant):
- Fluconazole 400 mg PO daily (antifungal; to be continued through engraftment, then reassessed per protocol)
- Acyclovir 800 mg PO BID or IV equivalent during aplasia (antiviral; convert to valacyclovir 500 mg BID once tolerating PO post-engraftment)
- TMP-SMX 1 DS tablet PO BID Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday (PCP prophylaxis; to be held during aplasia and restarted post-engraftment)
- Levofloxacin antibacterial prophylaxis during aplasia per institutional protocol

CMV Monitoring and Pre-emptive Therapy:
Given R+/D+ CMV serostatus (highest risk), weekly CMV PCR quantitative monitoring from peripheral blood will be initiated at day +7 and continued through approximately day +100 or until off immunosuppression. Letermovir prophylaxis may be considered per institutional protocol given high CMV reactivation risk; to be confirmed with infectious disease consult.

Stem Cell Infusion:
G-CSF mobilized peripheral blood stem cells from 10/10 MUD to be infused on day 0. Target CD34+ dose ≥5 x 10^6/kg.

Midostaurin:
Midostaurin continuation as bridge to transplant is under consideration. Current institutional practice and emerging data suggest FLT3 inhibition may be continued through conditioning until day -1 to maintain remission depth; this will be confirmed with the outpatient hematology team. Given the patient's excellent MRD-negative status, this is not urgently required but may be offered.

Logistical/Administrative:
- Patient and spouse have completed transplant education sessions (nursing and physician) regarding the transplant process, expected timeline, and anticipated complications
- Transplant consent signed
- Caregiver arrangements confirmed (spouse will serve as primary caregiver post-discharge)
- Temporary housing near transplant center arranged for the 100-day peri-transplant period
- Social work intake completed; psychosocial support resources provided; patient mildly anxious but coping well with support
- Dental clearance: completed (no active dental infection, no extractions required)
- Insurance/financial: pre-authorization confirmed through transplant billing department

Admission:
Planned inpatient admission in approximately 2 weeks for initiation of conditioning chemotherapy. Pre-admission CBC, CMP, and coagulation to be checked on day of admission. Central venous access (tunneled central line or apheresis catheter) to be placed by interventional radiology or surgery on admission.

Post-Transplant Surveillance Plan (overview discussed with patient):
- Engraftment assessment: CBC daily during aplasia; BMA/biopsy at day +30
- MRD and chimerism: day +30, +100, +180, 12 months by bone marrow
- Peripheral blood FLT3-ITD PCR: monthly for first 6 months, then quarterly
- CT imaging: planned at approximately day +90 and 12 months
- Tacrolimus monitoring: weekly initially, then with each clinic visit
- Late effects monitoring: endocrine, pulmonary, musculoskeletal, secondary malignancy, vaccination per protocol starting at 6 months post-transplant

Prognosis discussion (documented separately in detail):
Patient and spouse counseled regarding expected outcomes. With MRD-negative CR1 prior to myeloablative allo-HSCT from a 10/10 MUD, the estimated 2-year overall survival is approximately 50-65% for adverse-risk AML in this setting, with relapse remaining the primary risk. The graft-versus-leukemia (GVL) effect afforded by the allogeneic graft is a key component of the curative strategy. Risks including treatment-related mortality (estimated 10-15% for myeloablative MUD transplant in this age group and performance status), GVHD (acute GVHD grade II-IV approximately 30-50%; chronic GVHD approximately 40-60% at 1 year), infection, and other organ toxicities were thoroughly reviewed. Patient and spouse verbalized understanding and expressed commitment to proceeding.

Electronic documentation, nursing plan of care, and pre-transplant orders placed. Patient to return for admission as scheduled.

Attestation: I have personally seen and examined the patient, reviewed all available data including laboratory results, imaging, pathology, and molecular studies, and agree with the assessment and plan documented above.

— BMT Attending Physician, Hematology/Oncology — Stem Cell Transplant Program
